note: SNG-054 Phase 1 — the canonical TRADITION MAP. Abilities belong to a PEOPLE, not to an axis. Each of the 12 axes is the tension between two pole-civilizations, each with its craft. Replaces the 'Reach' grouping in the learn-screen/skill-graph. Authored by Aevi 2026-07-11 from the_twelve_reaches.json + world_framing.json canon; the 6 canon-named axes use their canon names, the 6 unnamed were authored here.
principle: A tradition is a PEOPLE's craft. You learn it by being native to that people, standing in their region, or being taught by one of them (teacher or tome). An axis is not a tradition — it is the tension between two of them.

architecture.poleTraditions: 24 (2 per axis × 12). The great crafts, practiced at the Reaches. 16 have abilities today; 8 are authored stubs owing trees.
architecture.folkTraditions: The Valley's near-center crafts — FOLK-SHADOWS of the great poles, diluted and mixed because the Valley sits near the center where all axes cross faintly. Canon: 'Prism Sight is folk-Radiance'; 'Harmonic Heights is a Valley-edge foothill leaning mechanical'; 'the Valley's Mediator's Tongue is a folk-shadow of Stillcraft'.
architecture.specialSystems: Outside the pole matrix: Precursor (ancient nanite-tech, fiction-gated).
architecture.cultsOfPurity: Each pole's PURE locus is held not by the civilization but by a cult (world_framing.bearers_vary) — the civilization lives at the livable near-pole; the cult occupies the extreme. Listed per tradition as cultOfPurity.

accessGates.principle: SNG-049/SNG-050 landing in mechanics — this closes the 'I can learn any Reach's capstone by picking it' hole. You may learn/rank a pole-tradition ability only if ONE of:
accessGates.native: your origin/lineage belongs to that people
accessGates.inRegion: you are currently in their region (their craft is active where they are)
accessGates.teacherOrTome: you hold a tome of that tradition, or have a teacher of that people (an NPC of the tradition, met and willing)
accessGates.folkTraditions: the Valley's folk traditions (harmonic/radiant/valley_craft) are OPEN — they are the near-center crossing; anyone in the Valley may learn them. This is what the center BEING able to hold everything means, mechanically.
accessGates.capstones: rank IV-V of a pole-tradition additionally requires deep standing with that people (teacher + reputation), not merely presence. Greatness is taught, not bought.

traditions.name: The Umbrals
traditions.civilization: The Umbrals — who see by other means in the great dark
traditions.cultOfPurity: those who would put out every light — the Unlit
traditions.aesthetic: lightless deeps, sight without light, soft-footed, everything felt not seen

traditions.name: The Blazeborn
traditions.civilization: The Blazeborn — who live in a light so total it has burned their land to glass
traditions.cultOfPurity: the Blaze itself — sterilizing, expanding, a light that has stopped loving what it shows
traditions.aesthetic: glass plains, revelation as violence, the burning truth, no shadow permitted

traditions.name: The Rootkin
traditions.civilization: The Rootkin — grove-cities that grow rather than build
traditions.cultOfPurity: growth that will not permit an ending — the Unceasing
traditions.aesthetic: grown not built, green-heart, sap and bark and slow enormous patience

traditions.name: The Ashwardens
traditions.civilization: The Ashwardens — who tend endings as the Rootkin tend growth
traditions.cultOfPurity: those who would end everything to end suffering — the Hollow
traditions.aesthetic: grey ash-fields, the mercy of stopping, quiet keepers of last things

traditions.name: The Marchers
traditions.civilization: The Marchers — a warrior culture whose whole society is honed conflict
traditions.cultOfPurity: violence for its own sake — the Redline's deepest cut
traditions.aesthetic: honed steel, the line that holds, scars worn as record

traditions.name: The Stillhold
traditions.civilization: The Stillhold — a walled peace so absolute it has become its own kind of prison
traditions.cultOfPurity: peace enforced by erasing the capacity for conflict — the Silent
traditions.aesthetic: white walls, unbroken quiet, a peace that has forgotten why it was needed

traditions.name: The Somatics
traditions.civilization: The Somatics — cultures of the perfected body, movement as language
traditions.cultOfPurity: the body perfected past personhood — the Sculpted
traditions.aesthetic: breath and sinew, movement as speech, the body as the only instrument

traditions.name: The Cogitants
traditions.civilization: The Cogitants — who have nearly left their bodies behind for pure thought, and pay for it
traditions.cultOfPurity: minds that have discarded the body entirely — the Discarnate
traditions.aesthetic: thin bodies, vast interiors, memory-palaces, thought as force

traditions.name: The Churnfolk
traditions.civilization: The Churnfolk — who live in constant productive disorder
traditions.cultOfPurity: dissolution for its own sake — those who ride the Churn until nothing holds
traditions.aesthetic: improvised everything, luck-riding, nothing twice, joyous mess

traditions.name: The Lattice-Cities
traditions.civilization: The Lattice-Cities — perfectly ordered arcologies where nothing is unplanned and nothing is free
traditions.cultOfPurity: order so total that freedom is a defect — the Grand Lattice's inner ring
traditions.aesthetic: arcologies, perfect prediction, the binding of possibility, no accident permitted

traditions.name: The Enginewrights
traditions.civilization: The Enginewrights — whose entire world is mechanism
traditions.cultOfPurity: those who would make everything a mechanism, including people — cf. Halvex Coil
traditions.aesthetic: gear-teeth and pressure, systems all the way down, the Precursor arts at their most technical

traditions.name: The Numinous
traditions.civilization: The Numinous — who have dissolved most of their material life into pure spirit-practice
traditions.cultOfPurity: those who have dissolved so far they can no longer return — the Unbodied
traditions.aesthetic: thinning veils, meaning without matter, the mystic disciplines the Valley only rumors

traditions.name: The Threnodists
traditions.civilization: The Threnodists — who hold that feeling is the only true knowing; their cities are built around grief-houses and joy-halls
traditions.cultOfPurity: feeling amplified past all judgment — the Raw
traditions.aesthetic: tidal, expressive, public weeping and public joy, architecture shaped for feeling

traditions.name: The Syllogists
traditions.civilization: The Syllogists — who reason from first principles and regard emotion as noise in the signal
traditions.cultOfPurity: reason purified of all feeling — the Bloodless
traditions.aesthetic: clean lines, argument as architecture, the cold pleasure of a proof

traditions.name: The Veilwrights
traditions.civilization: The Veilwrights — who hold that every truth is a made thing, and make better ones
traditions.cultOfPurity: falsehood so total it erases the possibility of truth — Halvex Coil's rewriting is its terminus
traditions.aesthetic: mirrors and masks, nothing quite where it appears, hospitality you cannot trust

traditions.name: The Verists
traditions.civilization: The Verists — who speak only what is so, whatever it costs, and it costs
traditions.cultOfPurity: truth wielded as violence — those who tell everything to everyone and call the ruin honesty
traditions.aesthetic: unadorned stone, no ornament, a truth that does not care whether you can bear it

traditions.name: The Abyssal Choir
traditions.civilization: The Abyssal Choir — who commune with what the world flinches from, and are not all wrong to
traditions.cultOfPurity: appetite without limit — the Devouring
traditions.aesthetic: horned architecture, bargains, an honesty about appetite the pious lack

traditions.name: The Seraphic Orders
traditions.civilization: The Seraphic Orders — luminous, hierarchical, certain; their mercy is real and so is their judgment
traditions.cultOfPurity: purity that would burn the impure — the Unfallen
traditions.aesthetic: tiered spires, unbearable choral certainty, mercy administered from above

traditions.name: The Masons of the Given
traditions.civilization: The Masons — who hold that only what can be touched is real; the world's great builders and its great skeptics
traditions.cultOfPurity: the real reduced to only the touchable — the Leaden
traditions.aesthetic: stone, weight, ledgers, a deep suspicion of anything that cannot be weighed

traditions.name: The Figurists
traditions.civilization: The Figurists — who work in pattern, symbol and idea, and hold matter to be the coarsest layer of the real
traditions.cultOfPurity: abstraction that has forgotten bodies suffer — the Untethered
traditions.aesthetic: impossible geometry, buildings that are arguments, a place that resists being mapped

traditions.name: The Horizon-Walkers
traditions.civilization: The Horizon-Walkers — nomads of distance; for them everywhere is a place you have not yet arrived
traditions.cultOfPurity: those who never arrive anywhere at all — the Unlanded
traditions.aesthetic: tents and roads, distance as home, a people with no fixed anything

traditions.name: The Hourkeepers
traditions.civilization: The Hourkeepers — who tend duration; their cities remember and pre-remember, and they pay for both
traditions.cultOfPurity: those who will not let a moment pass — the Stopped
traditions.aesthetic: clock-towers, archives that run forward, an unbearable patience

traditions.name: The Unmakers
traditions.civilization: The Unmakers — who hold that ending a thing well is a craft, and that the world is choked with what should have ended
traditions.cultOfPurity: unmaking as its own end — the Scouring
traditions.aesthetic: controlled ruin, beautiful demolitions, an ethic of clearing

traditions.name: The Wrights of the New
traditions.civilization: The Wrights — who cannot stop making; their cities are never finished and that is the point
traditions.cultOfPurity: making that cannot stop to ask whether it should — the Ceaseless
traditions.aesthetic: scaffolds forever, half-built cathedrals, the joy and the waste of it

folkTraditions.name: The Harmonic
folkTraditions.aesthetic: resonance-instruments, tuned stone, sound as craft

access.note: Valley folk-tradition — open to anyone in the Valley

folkTraditions.note: Canon: 'Harmonic Heights is a Valley-edge foothill leaning mechanical.' A folk-shadow of Enginecraft/Latticework, in the medium of sound.

folkTraditions.name: The Radiant
folkTraditions.aesthetic: prisms, lenses, worked light — revelation as craft, not as burning

access.note: Valley folk-tradition — open to anyone in the Valley

folkTraditions.note: Canon: 'Radiant Plateau is a Valley-edge foothill of the light end'; 'Prism Sight is folk-Radiance.' The gentle, worked version of the Blaze's burning revelation.

folkTraditions.name: Valley Craft
folkTraditions.aesthetic: hearth, road, river, workbench — the crafts of people who must actually live
folkTraditions.note: The near-center crossing made craft. Canon: 'the Valley's Mediator's Tongue is a folk-shadow of Stillcraft.' Its breadth IS its identity — the center holds a little of everything.

specialSystems.name: Precursor Arts

access.note: fiction-gated: requires precursor contact/marking, not native/region/teacher

specialSystems.note: OUTSIDE the pole matrix — not an axis-people but the substrate the whole world is built on. Halvex Coil's mastery of it is why he is arc-scale.

crossPoleBraids.note: Abilities that sit BETWEEN the two poles of their axis — they belong to neither people and can only be reached by holding both. These are the seeds of the combination system (SNG-054 combination pass) and are DELIBERATELY hard to learn: they require standing in BOTH traditions.

abilities.name: The Harbored Flame
abilities.why: a light you carry hidden — conceal+heal. Neither the Umbrals (who would put it out) nor the Blazeborn (who would let it burn everything) can make this alone.

abilities.name: The Meaning-Engine
abilities.why: a mechanism that MEANS — mend+reveal. The Enginewrights build it; only the Numinous can make it mean.

abilities.name: The Turning Word
abilities.why: the argument that also heals — bind+reveal+heal. Reason alone cannot turn a heart; feeling alone cannot show it why.

migration.note: CCode: the learn-screen and skill-graph must group by traditionId from THIS file, not by the reach_* source files. Existing characters' learned abilities MIGRATE by ability id → traditionId lookup; nobody loses a skill. Source ability files may stay as-is (grouping is a content-layer concern) or be reorganized later — the engine should read groupings from traditions.json.
migration.abilityToTradition: derive from traditions[].abilities + folkTraditions[].abilities + specialSystems[].abilities + crossPoleBraids

owed.corpusDepth: The 8 newly-built pole-traditions have anchor + core techniques (full rank I–III). They can be DEEPENED later (more techniques, capstones) as their regions come into play — but every tradition is now playable.
owed.combinationPass: COMPLETE for the current corpus (44 recipes, tiered). New combos accrue as the corpus deepens.
